findings.message: This section still advertises the deleted daemon task `cargo make test-one-exec`, so the harness-policy docs point users at a command that no longer exists. Update it to the new run-`--rm` wrapper or remove the example.

Stale `cargo make track-local-review` guidance remains after the migration. That wrapper is no longer defined, so the agent now points at a dead command; the same stale reference also appears in `.claude/rules/07-dev-environment.md:15`.

The direct `bin/sotp review local` example still includes a `--` separator before the flags. `review local` takes its flags directly, so this command will not parse; the same mistake also appears in `.claude/rules/10-guardrails.md:82-83` for the planner/reviewer examples.

The PermissionRequest hook still tells users to fall back to `cargo make track-review-results`, `cargo make track-check-approved`, and `cargo make track-local-plan` for direct native review/plan commands, but those cargo-make tasks are no longer defined and were removed from the allowlists. That leaves the newly documented `bin/sotp review results` / `bin/sotp review check-approved` / `bin/sotp plan codex-local` paths blocked with no valid fallback.

The fallback example for the reviewer is not runnable as written: `bin/sotp review local` requires `--round-type` and `--group`, but the guardrail only shows `--model` and `--briefing-file`. Copying this snippet will fail argument parsing instead of launching the reviewer.

The reviewer invocation example is stale: the real fixer prompt prepends `bin/sotp track views sync &&` and passes `--track-id`, but this section shows only `bin/sotp review local --round-type fast --group {scope} --briefing-file ...` and says the reviewer self-resolves track-id. That misstates the command contract and can lead agents to omit the sync prelude.

`sotp review local` does not invoke `track type-signals`; only the commit-gate `track-active-gate` does. This note overstates the host-side `protoc` requirement for review runs and ties it to the wrong command path.

This still refers to `sotp review codex-local`, but the current entry point is `bin/sotp review local`. The old command name is no longer valid, so this guidance now points agents at a non-existent command.

This note still points to `track-pr-merge`, but that cargo-make wrapper no longer exists; the merge path has been renamed to `bin/sotp pr wait-and-merge` / `/track:merge`. Update the note so it does not reference a dead command.

PermissionRequest still tells users to fall back to `cargo make track-check-approved` and `cargo make track-review-results`, but this patch removed those wrappers from the allowlist and Makefile, so the hook now points at dead commands for `bin/sotp review check-approved` / `results`.

The Codex dry-fix wrapper example is missing the `--` separator before forwarded args. Without it, `--track-id` and `--briefing-file` are treated as cargo-make flags instead of reaching `track-local-dry-fix`.

Both Codex review-fix examples dropped the `--` separator (`cargo make track-local-review-fix-codex --scope ...`), so `--scope` / `--track-id` / `--briefing-file` will not be forwarded to the wrapper. The fast-round example at this line and the final-round example at line 127 both need the separator restored.

The generated Claude-provider reviewer prompt gives the agent a single `bin/sotp track views sync && bin/sotp review local ...` command, but `.claude/settings.json` now denies any Bash permission request containing `&&` before considering alternatives. Since this is presented as the reviewer invocation, the review-fix agent can be blocked before recording the round. Split this into a separate sync instruction plus a standalone `bin/sotp review local ...` invocation.

This still contains the placeholder command reference `cargo make track-...` in the completed-state verification path. That wrapper does not exist and this migration is replacing these passthrough references with native `bin/sotp` calls, so an agent following the first command in the sentence can run an invalid verification command. Replace the placeholder with only `bin/sotp dry check-approved --track-id <id>`.

The migration from `cargo make track-local-review` to `bin/sotp review local` drops the mandatory pre-review `track-active-gate`. The removed wrapper used to run type-signals, catalogue-spec-signals, and views sync before review; `bin/sotp review local` does not do that itself, and this doc now only suggests an optional `bin/sotp track views sync`. That can record review rounds against stale generated artifacts/signals and later fail approval/commit on stale hashes. Make the full native pre-review gate mandatory before each review round, as separate commands: `bin/sotp track type-signals`, `bin/sotp track catalogue-spec-signals`, then `bin/sotp track views sync`.

D5 states that `verify-*` Docker reproducibility gates remain as `cargo make` entry points, but D7 later says the public `verify-doc-links` wrapper should be deleted and only the internal dependency retained. Since `verify-doc-links` is itself a `verify-*` task, the plan gives conflicting implementation requirements unless D5 excludes this task or D7 is explicitly marked as an exception.

D6's concrete sequence requires step 2 to run `cargo make ci`, and D5 explicitly keeps CI as a `cargo make` Docker reproducibility gate, but this summary says the script will contain only “6 native commands.” That creates a semantic conflict about the layer placement of CI: implementers cannot both keep `cargo make ci` in the sequence and make every remaining step a native subcommand.

D9 conflates `.harness/config/dry-check.json` as both the source of truth for the DRY threshold and a historical record whose `threshold` value should not affect the next decision. Those roles conflict in the same file/field, so the implementation boundary is ambiguous unless config and history are split or the wording is tightened.

T003 explicitly leaves `MakeTask::Exec` intact, but IN-01/AC-01 require `bin/sotp make exec` to be removed as part of the daemon teardown, and task-coverage maps AC-01 only to T003. As written, the plan can complete T003 while the AC-01 command surface remains until T004, so the task/coverage contract contradicts the spec.

The task order deletes the entire `bin/sotp make` command in T004 before T005 removes non-git cargo-make wrappers and before T006 rewires git-write wrappers. T004 still claims CI must pass after that commit, but the plan leaves wrapper tasks scheduled for later that still depend on the deleted intermediate command, making the per-task green-state plan infeasible.

IN-04 includes the `add` git-write task, but T006's concrete rewrite list omits `add`, and task-coverage maps IN-04/AC-04 only to T006. That leaves the `add` wrapper outside the implementation plan despite being in scope.

IN-10 requires both adding `.harness/config/dry-check.json` and removing the scattered `0.85` hardcodes plus `resolve_commit_dry_threshold`, but task-coverage maps IN-10 only to T001 while T001 explicitly says existing `0.85` defaults remain. The hardcode removal is in T007, so IN-10 is under-covered as recorded.

`T004` assumes all `bin/sotp make` callers are gone before `apps/cli/src/commands/make.rs` is deleted, but `T008` is still the task that rewrites `commit` / `track-commit-message`. Until `T008` runs, those commit-related tasks remain the remaining callers, so deleting the subcommand at this point would strand them and break CI. Reorder the plan so the commit-script rebuild happens before `T004`, or fold that rewrite into the same step.

T005 claims that after the non-git passthrough migration no Makefile.toml non-git route still calls `bin/sotp make`, and T004 relies on that before deleting the make subcommand. However spec IN-03 includes `track-baseline-capture` in the non-git passthrough set, while the plan leaves that wrapper removal to T009, which runs after T004. This makes the dependency order internally inconsistent: T004 can delete `bin/sotp make` while the `track-baseline-capture` cargo-make wrapper still exists and may still route through it.

`track-signals` has two different removal points in the plan: T005 already removes it from `Makefile.toml` (`plan.md:26`), but S6/S8 still describe it as a wrapper that survives until T009 (`plan.md:46`, `plan.md:60`). T009's actual task body only removes `track-baseline-capture` (`plan.md:63`), so the wrapper's lifecycle is internally contradictory.

CN-03 requires any cargo make task that invokes `bin/sotp git <sub>` to use `command`/`args` arrays and explicitly not an `@shell` script, but AC-06 and T008 require `commit`/`track-commit-message` to be a 6-step `@shell` script that invokes `bin/sotp git add-all` and `bin/sotp git commit-from-file`. As written, the plan cannot satisfy both the constraint and the commit-script acceptance/task requirements; CN-03 needs an explicit D6 orchestration exception or the T008/AC-06 design needs to change.

The verifier now requires the snippet `bin/sotp review local -- --round-type fast --group`, but the migrated native CLI does not use the cargo-make passthrough separator. The actual `sotp review local` clap command expects flags directly after `local`, and `.claude/commands/track/review.md` already documents `bin/sotp review local --round-type ...`, so `verify-orchestra` will report the review guidance as missing even when the docs are correct. The same stale `-- --model` pattern appears in the adjacent codex-system snippets and should be removed from the native command expectations.

Switching the fixer prompt from `cargo make track-local-review` to `bin/sotp review local` drops the old cargo-make task dependency on `track-active-gate` (`type-signals -> catalogue-spec-signals -> views sync`) before every canonical review. I could not find equivalent regeneration in the current `sotp review local` path, so review-fix rounds can be recorded against stale rendered views/signals, which can leave review approval/hash state inconsistent with the later commit gate.

Replacing the reviewer command with direct `bin/sotp review local` drops the `track-local-review` wrapper's `track-active-gate` dependency. I don't see an equivalent pre-review `type-signals -> catalogue-spec-signals -> views sync` step in `sotp review local`, so fixer rounds can record review hashes against stale rendered views and later fail check-approved with a stale hash. The native review path needs to run the same gate before invoking/recording the reviewer, or this prompt should still point at a gated wrapper.

These required snippets no longer match the documented codex-system commands. The current `.claude/skills/codex-system/SKILL.md` contains `bin/sotp review local --round-type ... --model ...`, while the verifier now requires `bin/sotp review local -- --model ...`; the literal check in `verify_review_wrapper_guidance` will therefore report the guidance as missing. The extra `--` is also inconsistent with the `sotp review local` clap options, so the verifier should require the real command shape.

The new CLI entry point has no happy-path or failure-path tests in the changed files. That leaves `TrackCommand::SetCommitHash` / `execute_set_commit_hash` unverified for the required branch-resolution and recovery-hint behavior.

`execute_set_commit_hash` forwards `outcome.stderr` even though `CliApp::track_set_commit_hash` already writes the same error and recovery hint directly to stderr. On failures this emits an extra bare error line, and the `make` wrapper now inherits that changed output contract instead of a pure delegation.

The new CLI entry point has no happy-path or failure-path tests in the changed files. That leaves `TrackCommand::SetCommitHash` / `execute_set_commit_hash` unverified for the required branch-resolution and recovery-hint behavior.

`execute_set_commit_hash` forwards `outcome.stderr` even though `CliApp::track_set_commit_hash` already writes the same error and recovery hint directly to stderr. On failures this emits an extra bare error line, and the `make` wrapper now inherits that changed output contract instead of a pure delegation.

Anchoring the new native subcommand to `PathBuf::from("track/items")` makes `bin/sotp track set-commit-hash` fail before the composition layer runs whenever it is launched from a repo subdirectory, because the path is resolved against the current working directory instead of the discovered repo root. The underlying `CliApp::track_set_commit_hash` path already works from the repo root regardless of cwd, so this regresses a valid invocation context.

`run_in_dir` changes the process-wide current directory and only restores it after the closure returns normally. If the closure panics, Rust unwinds past the restore call, leaving later tests running from the temp repo directory (which may then be deleted), so tests using this shared helper are no longer independent. Restore via a guard/drop or `catch_unwind` so cleanup happens on both success and panic paths.

`cargo make track-sync-views` is still documented as the primary way to generate `plan.md` in both the SSoT and lifecycle sections, but that wrapper task was deleted from `Makefile.toml` in this patch. Following the workflow now points users at a non-existent command; the doc should be updated to only reference `bin/sotp track views sync`.

The `track-active-gate` / `track-commit-message` descriptions still mention `track-local-review`, but that wrapper was removed from the same change. The Makefile's own help text now advertises a task path that no longer exists, which leaves stale migration guidance in the canonical task list.

This canonical review-fix doc still names deleted wrapper tasks (`track-check-approved` here, and `track-sync-views` a few lines below). The workflow now routes through `bin/sotp review check-approved` and `bin/sotp track views sync`, so leaving the old task names is a broken ref in the instructions that drive the review gate.

The `track-commit-message` description still says the regen sequence is shared with `track-local-review`, but that wrapper was removed in this change set. The description no longer matches the actual native `review local` path, so this leaves a stale reference in a task that is still part of the workflow.

The native `bin/sotp review local` examples still include the cargo-make argument separator and omit required native options. The CLI rejects arguments after `--` (`bin/sotp review local -- --help` fails with `unexpected argument`), and `review local` requires `--round-type` and `--group`; the repeated examples at lines 197, 272, and 288 have the same issue. Users following these snippets will not be able to run the local reviewer loop after the wrapper removal.

`bin/sotp review local` is documented here as running `bin/sotp track views sync` before recording the round, but the native review path only dispatches the reviewer and writes `review.json`; it does not run the old `track-active-gate` dependency that the removed `cargo make track-local-review` wrapper provided. A review-fix lead following this skill can now record zero findings against stale generated views, then the commit path regenerates views and `check-approved` blocks on a stale scope hash.

The requested deletion list still has live Makefile task definitions for `track-local-review-fix-codex` and `track-local-dry-fix`. The briefing/checklist says all 17 target tasks must be absent, so keeping these wrappers leaves the migration incomplete and preserves deprecated cargo-make entry points.

The wrapper tests now explicitly require `track-local-review-fix-codex` and `track-local-dry-fix` to exist, even though those tasks are in the requested deletion set. Once the Makefile is corrected, this test will fail unless the assertion is removed or inverted.

The deleted passthrough-task checks were removed, but this file no longer asserts the two retained `track-local-review-fix-codex` / `track-local-dry-fix` Makefile wrappers added in `Makefile.toml`. A regression in either CODEX_BIN shell wrapper would now slip past `scripts/test_make_wrappers.py`, so the retained tasks need explicit coverage.
